matches.x: 1 TO seal the testimony of this book and the Book of Mormon, we announce the martyrdom of Joseph Smith the Prophet, and Hyrum Smith the Patriarch. They were shot in Carthage jail, on the 27th of June, 1844, about five o'clock p.m., by an armed mob--painted black--of from 150 to 200 persons. Hyrum was shot first and fell calmly, exclaiming: I am a dead man! Joseph leaped from the window, and was…

John may have seen the martyrs Joseph Smith and Hyrum Smith under the altar. John Taylor wrote that the "innocent blood" of Joseph and Hyrum, "with the innocent blood of all the martyrs under the altar that John saw, will cry unto the Lord of Hosts till he avenges that blood on the earth" (D&C 135:7). (Donald W. Parry and Jay A. Parry, Understanding the Book of Revelation [Salt Lake City: Deseret…
